# Misty Step

- **Level**: 2
- **School**: Conjuration
- **Casting Time**: 1 bonus action
- **Range**: Self
- **Components**: V
- **Duration**: Instantaneous
- **Classes**: Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard, Fey Wanderer Ranger,
  Oath of the Ancients Paladin

## Description

Briefly surrounded by silvery mist, you teleport up to 30 feet to an
unoccupied space that you can see.

## Tactical Notes

- **Bonus action teleport**: Misty Step is the standard "escape
  grapple" or "reposition" spell.  Because it costs only a bonus
  action, you can still cast a cantrip (Fire Bolt, Eldritch Blast)
  with your action on the same turn.
- **30 feet is half a battle map tile**: Plenty to break a melee
  ring or cross a chasm.  Not enough to fully escape a chasing
  enemy with 30 feet of movement and a Dash.
- **Line of sight requirement**: You must see the destination.  This
  prevents teleporting through walls or into sealed rooms — a hard
  counter to ambush setups.
- **Verbal component**: You must speak.  Silenced characters cannot
  Misty Step.  The flavor text implies the mist is briefly visible,
  so stealthy escapes are not possible.
- **Grapple escape**: Teleporting ends any grapple affecting you
  because the grappler is no longer in range.  This is the cheapest
  counter to high-Str monsters that pin spellcasters.

## Cross-References

- See `feather-fall.md` for the reaction that pairs with Misty Step
  when escaping traps: Misty Step out of the hazard, Feather Fall
  if the hazard is a pit.
